categories.name: Runtime Errors
categories.description: Errors that occur during the execution of a program, leading to unexpected termination or crashes. Examples include NameError, TypeError, IndexError in Python, and Segmentation Faults, Division by Zero in C++.

categories.name: Compiler & Syntax Errors
categories.description: Errors detected by the parser or compiler before the program can run. Includes missing colons/semicolons, undefined references, and type mismatch errors during compilation.

categories.name: Logical Bugs
categories.description: Code that executes without crashing but produces incorrect or unintended results. Includes off-by-one errors, incorrect boolean logic, variable shadowing, and unintended state mutations.

categories.name: Optimization & Performance
categories.description: Functionally correct code that suffers from sub-optimal time or space complexity. Examples include O(N) popping from lists, deep copying large objects, and recomputing loop invariants.

categories.name: Educational Explanations
categories.description: Conceptual explanations of core programming paradigms and language-specific features rather than explicit bug fixes. Covers generators, smart pointers, RAII, decorators, and object instantiation.
